title: Competition among Aedes aegypti larvae in microcosms
author: Kurt Steinwascher
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.tcweixe

steps.title: For experiment 1
steps.instruction: For experiment 1: Obtain mosquito eggs from an A. aegypti colony after feeding females on a mouse.

steps.title: Hatch the eggs in distilled water,...
steps.instruction: Hatch the eggs in distilled water, immersing them for at least 2 hours.

steps.title: Assign random numbers to treatments
steps.instruction: Assign random numbers to treatments: 20 treatments (4 food levels crossed with 5 densities, see below) with 5 replicates each.

steps.title: Number 100 flat-bottomed shel vials (25...
steps.instruction: Number 100 flat-bottomed shel vials (25 mm diameter x 95 mm tall) with random number and treatment.

steps.title: Fill shell vials with 20 ml distilled water in each
steps.instruction: Fill shell vials with 20 ml distilled water in each.

steps.title: Weigh out baker’s yeast and dissolve...
steps.instruction: Weigh out baker’s yeast and dissolve it in distilled water to achieve the following concentrations: 2 mg/larva, 3 mg/larva, 4 mg/larva and 5 mg/larva crossed with 5 densities (4, 5, 6, 7 and 8 larvae per vial). [2 mg/larva with 4 larva per vial requires a total amount of yeast of 8 mg, dissolved in 0.5 ml distilled water and added to the 20 ml in the appropriate vials—5 replicates of each treatment, for 20 treatments.]

steps.title: Count 4, 5, 6, 7 or...
steps.instruction: Count 4, 5, 6, 7 or 8 larvae into each vial according to the treatment marked on the vial.

steps.title: Arrange vials by number (randomized sequence)...
steps.instruction: Arrange vials by number (randomized sequence) and cover with cheese cloth to prevent food or other mosquitoes from entering the vials.

steps.title: Store vials in a room at...
steps.instruction: Store vials in a room at ambient temperatures (in Florida) with a temperature range of 18 degrees C to 33 degrees C.

steps.title: Day zero is the day the experiment begins
steps.instruction: Day zero is the day the experiment begins. Check the vials for pupae beginning on day 4 (this is the day before any larvae are expected to pupate).

steps.title: Remove each pupa into a holding...
steps.instruction: Remove each pupa into a holding shell vial with 10 ml distilled water. Record the original vial number, the treatment, the date (day of pupation). To pick up a pupa without disturbing the treatment vial, use a fire-polished wide mouth glass pipette (about 2 mm diameter) to capture the pupa at the water surface and transfer it to the holding vial. Capillary forces will capture the pupa, but you may have to gently blow the pupa out of the tube into the holding vial. Put only 1 pupa in each holding vial.

steps.title: Remove each pupa from the holding...
steps.instruction: Remove each pupa from the holding vial by pipette as described, blot it on a piece of paper towel, pick it up gently with fine-tipped forceps and place it on a balance. Weigh it to the nearest 0.01 mg. Record the weight. Transfer it to the stage of a 10 x stereoscopic microscope and determine the sex of the pupa. Record the sex. Transfer it back to a vial of water to complete pupation (and eventually to rejoin the mosquito colony).

steps.title: Continue until all larvae have pupated or died
steps.instruction: Continue until all larvae have pupated or died.

steps.title: For experiment 2
steps.instruction: For experiment 2: Obtain mosquito eggs from an A. aegypti colony after feeding females on a mouse.

steps.title: Hatch the eggs in distilled water,...
steps.instruction: Hatch the eggs in distilled water, immersing them for at least 2 hours.

steps.title: Assign random numbers to treatments
steps.instruction: Assign random numbers to treatments: 4 treatments (2 food levels crossed with 2 densities, see below) with 40 replicates each.

steps.title: Number 160 flat-bottomed shel vials (25...
steps.instruction: Number 160 flat-bottomed shel vials (25 mm diameter x 95 mm tall) with random number and treatment.

steps.title: Fill shell vials with 20 ml distilled water in each
steps.instruction: Fill shell vials with 20 ml distilled water in each.

steps.title: Weigh out baker’s yeast and dissolve...
steps.instruction: Weigh out baker’s yeast and dissolve in distilled water to achieve the following concentrations: 3 mg/larva and 4 mg/larva crossed with 2 densities (5 and 6 larvae per vial). [3 mg/larva with 5 larva per vial requires a total amount of yeast of 15 mg, dissolved in 0.5 ml distilled water and added to the 20 ml in the appropriate vials—40 replicates of each treatment, for 4 treatments.]

steps.title: Count 5 or 6 larvae into...
steps.instruction: Count 5 or 6 larvae into each vial according to the treatment marked on the vial.

steps.title: Arrange vials by number (randomized sequence)...
steps.instruction: Arrange vials by number (randomized sequence) and cover with cheese cloth to prevent food or other mosquitoes from entering the vials.

steps.title: Store vials in an insectary at...
steps.instruction: Store vials in an insectary at 26 degrees C and 12/12 light/dark cycle for the first four days and overnight thereafter.

steps.title: Day zero is the day the experiment begins
steps.instruction: Day zero is the day the experiment begins. Check the vials for pupae beginning on day 4 (this is the day before any larvae are expected to pupate).

steps.title: Remove each pupa into a holding...
steps.instruction: Remove each pupa into a holding shell vial with 10 ml distilled water. Record the original vial number, the treatment, the date (day of pupation). To pick up a pupa without disturbing the treatment vial, use a fire-polished wide mouth glass pipette (about 2 mm diameter) to capture the pupa at the water surface and transfer it to the holding vial. Capillary forces will capture the pupa, but you may have to gently blow the pupa out of the tube into the holding vial. Put only 1 pupa in each holding vial.

steps.title: Remove each pupa from the holding...
steps.instruction: Remove each pupa from the holding vial by pipette as described, blot it on a piece of paper towel, pick it up gently with fine-tipped forceps and place it on a balance. Weigh it to the nearest 0.01 mg. Record the weight. Transfer it to the stage of a 10 x stereoscopic microscope and determine the sex of the pupa. Record the sex. Transfer it back to a vial of water to complete pupation (and eventually to rejoin the mosquito colony).

steps.title: Continue until all larvae have pupated or died
steps.instruction: Continue until all larvae have pupated or died.
